coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] exact remedy row content in architecture.md will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: exact remedy row content in architecture.md

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update docs/architecture.md's fsck.py remedy row to match the plan's full MOVE list (including _ticket_dirs, _active_snapshots, _has_remote, module-level _git), or acknowledge in the plan that the remedy row is being superseded/expanded and explicitly state which additional symbols are being moved beyond what the row names.
evidence: Plan body: 'the plan MATCHES the recorded row; no deviation, and the row is deleted on graduation'

docs/architecture.md line 266 remedy text: 'extract the repair cluster (_repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _repair_run, the schedule-disable/enable helpers) into a sibling fsck_repair.py, leaving the diagnostic scan + _transform_json in fsck.py'
Plan body MOVE list also includes: '_ticket_dirs', '_active_snapshots', '_has_remote', module-level '_git'
_ticket_dirs is called by _scan at fsck.py lines 108, 130, 188, 244 (staying in fsck.py), so moving it changes the dependency topology vs. what the remedy row describes
findings.finding: The plan claims 'The docs/architecture.md offenders remedy row already names this exact seam' but the actual remedy row (docs/architecture.md line 266) lists only '_repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _repair_run, the schedule-disable/enable helpers' — it does not name _ticket_dirs, _active_snapshots, _has_remote, or the module-level _git as part of the moved cluster. The plan moves more symbols than the remedy row describes, so the 'exact seam' claim is overstated.
findings.location: Plan body: 'the plan MATCHES the recorded row' / docs/architecture.md line 266
scenarios: A reviewer cross-checks the plan against architecture.md and flags _ticket_dirs/_active_snapshots/_has_remote as 'not in the remedy row' — triggering a scope dispute mid-implementation.
The architecture.md row is updated to match the new (expanded) scope, but without an explicit plan note this update is implicit and may be missed.

evidence: verify command `wc -l` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC1's LEAF property, e.g. `python -c 'import ast, pathlib; src=pathlib.Path("src/rebar/_commands/fsck_repair.py").read_text(); assert "rebar._commands.fsck" not in src, "circular import detected"'` or equivalent grep.
evidence: AC1 text: 'it is a LEAF (imports nothing from rebar._commands.fsck)' — no grep / python -c / ast check is commanded
The test suite in AC5 does not exercise import-graph topology
findings.finding: AC1's LEAF constraint ('it is a LEAF (imports nothing from rebar._commands.fsck)') has no concrete proving command, so an executor cannot mechanically verify the import direction is correct after the split.
scenarios: An accidental back-import (e.g. a helper call left in fsck_repair.py) would create a circular import that crashes at runtime; no AC command catches it before merge.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'Verified with: `grep -r "from rebar._commands.fsck" src/rebar/_commands/fsck_repair.py` returns no output.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a short module-level docstring (or a `# Architecture:` comment block) to both `fsck_repair.py` and `fsck.py` stating the one-way import contract (fsck_repair.py is a leaf; fsck.py re-exports moved names for back-compat) so the invariant survives deletion of the architecture.md remedy row.
evidence: 'its offenders remedy row removed from docs/architecture.md'
'fsck.py re-imports (re-exports) the moved names … carry # noqa: F401'
ABSENCE: the plan does not mention adding module-level docstrings or inline comments to either file explaining the leaf constraint or the re-export rationale
findings.finding: After the architecture.md remedy row is deleted (AC3), there is no persisted record of the one-way import contract (fsck_repair.py must remain a leaf; fsck.py re-exports for back-compat). Future maintainers encountering # noqa: F401 re-exports in fsck.py will have no in-codebase explanation of the invariant.
findings.location: Plan body ('the row is deleted on graduation') + AC3 + src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py (re-export block)
scenarios: A future developer adds an import of fsck.py into fsck_repair.py, creating a circular dependency, because no in-file comment states the leaf constraint
A reviewer sees # noqa: F401 re-exports and removes them during a cleanup pass, breaking the import contract for external callers
findings.suggested_fix:
In fsck_repair.py top-of-file:
```python
"""
fsck_repair — repair/remediation cluster extracted from fsck.py.

Architecture note: this module is a LEAF — it must not import from
rebar._commands.fsck. fsck.py re-exports the symbols moved here for
back-compat; see docs/architecture.md history for rationale.
"""
```
In fsck.py re-export block:
```python
# Back-compat re-exports: these names moved to fsck_repair.py.
# Keep this block so 'from rebar._commands.fsck import <symbol>' and
# 'fsck.<symbol>' attribute access continue to resolve. noqa: F401
from rebar._commands.fsck_repair import (  # noqa: F401
    _AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES,
    _HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES,
    _repair_plan,
    _repair_ticket,
    _repair_run,
)
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC2's '5 back-compat names' count with the 6 symbols the body identifies as moved (including `_ticket_dirs`); state the exact list in AC2 so the count and the symbol set agree.
evidence: AC2: 're-exports the 5 back-compat names'
Plan body: '_ticket_dirs`+`_resolve_tracker_git_dir` move to the leaf … fsck.py re-imports from the leaf'
Plan body names 5 re-export-targeted symbols but separately calls out _ticket_dirs as also re-imported
findings.finding: AC2 states 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names' but the plan body names 6 symbols moved to the leaf that fsck.py must re-import: `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_ticket_dirs`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`. The off-by-one creates ambiguity about whether `_ticket_dirs` requires a re-export entry or not.
scenarios: An executing agent omits the `_ticket_dirs` re-export because AC2 says '5'; any caller doing `from rebar._commands.fsck import _ticket_dirs` breaks at runtime.
An agent includes 6 re-exports but AC2's count check fails a mechanical verifier.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC2 to: 'fsck.py re-exports the 6 back-compat names (`_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_ticket_dirs`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`), the latter four carrying # noqa: F401.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for AC2's re-export requirement, e.g. `python -c 'from rebar._commands.fsck import _resolve_tracker_git_dir, _ticket_dirs, _repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES, _HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES'` (adjust list to match the reconciled count from the E2 finding above).
evidence: AC2 ends after stating the wc -l bound and re-export requirement — no python -c or grep command is provided
The pytest suites in AC5 may exercise some re-exports indirectly but do not constitute a targeted import-graph check
findings.finding: AC2's re-export claim ('it re-exports the N back-compat names') has no proving command; there is no import-check commanded that would verify the re-exports are actually present and importable from fsck.py.
scenarios: A re-export line accidentally omitted or misspelled would not be caught by wc -l or lint; the test suite might still pass if the test fixtures use direct imports from fsck_repair.py.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC2: 'Verified with: `python -c "from rebar._commands.fsck import _resolve_tracker_git_dir, _ticket_dirs, _repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES, _HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES"` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC2 (or the plan body) how `_ticket_dirs`'s re-import in fsck.py should be treated: either count it as a 6th re-imported name (and specify whether it needs `# noqa: F401` since it is used internally by fsck.py's diagnostic functions, making F401 inapplicable), or explicitly state it is an internal import only with no external consumers and thus needs no noqa annotation.
evidence: Plan body: '`_ticket_dirs`+`_resolve_tracker_git_dir` move to the leaf (shared by both diagnostic + repair; moving them keeps imports one-way: fsck.py re-imports from the leaf)'
AC2: 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names'
Plan body: '`_repair_plan`/`_repair_ticket`/`_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`/`_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES` are re-export-only (test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py) and carry # noqa: F401'
ABSENCE: `_ticket_dirs` is not listed among the 5 named back-compat re-exports and no `# noqa: F401` treatment is specified for it
findings.finding: AC2 says fsck.py 're-exports the 5 back-compat names', but the plan body also states that `_ticket_dirs` moves to fsck_repair.py with fsck.py re-importing it for its retained diagnostic functions — making the actual re-imported count 6. The plan does not specify whether `_ticket_dirs`'s re-import in fsck.py requires `# noqa: F401` (to pass lint / AC6) or whether it is treated purely as an internal import. This ambiguity could cause an implementer to either miss adding the noqa comment (breaking AC6) or incorrectly add it (implying back-compat semantics that weren't intended).
findings.location: Plan body ('`_ticket_dirs`+`_resolve_tracker_git_dir` move to the leaf') vs. AC2 ('re-exports the 5 back-compat names') vs. Plan body ('# noqa: F401' applied only to 4 repair names)
scenarios: Implementer adds `from rebar._commands.fsck_repair import _ticket_dirs` to fsck.py for internal use without `# noqa: F401`; ruff flags it as an unused import; `make lint` fails, breaking AC6.

Implementer assumes `_ticket_dirs` is one of the 5 re-exports and adds `# noqa: F401`, but ruff still flags it if the name IS used internally (noqa unnecessary); or the count discrepancy causes confusion about what AC2 is verifying.
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC2 to: 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names (with `# noqa: F401`) AND re-imports `_ticket_dirs` for internal use (no noqa needed — it is consumed by the retained diagnostic functions).' This disambiguates the two classes of moved names.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC2's '5 back-compat names' to enumerate the complete set of re-exported names (or give the actual count) so the acceptance criterion is unambiguously verifiable and implementers don't under-export.
evidence: AC2: 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names'

test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py:133,147,164 accesses fsck._repair_plan; lines 178,195 accesses fsck._repair_ticket

tests/interfaces/store/test_stale_lock_reclaim_write.py:57, test_event_append_transient_retry.py:227, test_concurrency_regression.py:750 each import _resolve_tracker_git_dir from rebar._commands.fsck
findings.finding: AC2 asserts 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names' but the plan body enumerates at least 12 symbols moved to fsck_repair.py that fsck.py must re-export to preserve back-compat: _resolve_tracker_git_dir, _ticket_dirs, _active_snapshots, _repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES, _HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES, _has_remote, _reconciler_pause, _reconciler_resume, _reconciler_in_flight, _repair_run. The number '5' is not grounded in the plan body and the discrepancy could cause an implementer to omit re-exports, silently breaking consumers that import from rebar._commands.fsck.
findings.location: AC2 ('it re-exports the 5 back-compat names') vs. plan body (MOVE list)
scenarios: An implementer reading only AC2 re-exports only 5 names, omitting e.g. _active_snapshots or _has_remote — any caller importing those from fsck gets an AttributeError at runtime.
The AC2 verifier checks for exactly '5 back-compat names' in fsck.py and flags the implementation as non-conformant when the correct count of re-exports lands, stalling the review.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '5 back-compat names' with the full enumeration matching the plan's MOVE list, e.g.: 'it re-exports all moved names for back-compat: _resolve_tracker_git_dir, _ticket_dirs, _active_snapshots, _repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES, _HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES, _has_remote, _reconciler_pause, _reconciler_resume, _reconciler_in_flight, _repair_run (and the module-level _git if callers access it externally) — each carrying # noqa: F401 where unused in fsck.py itself.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC2's '5 back-compat names' count with the plan body's move list: either explicitly add `_ticket_dirs` to the re-import list in AC2 (as a 6th symbol, distinguished as an internal re-import rather than an external re-export), or note in AC2 that `_ticket_dirs` is re-imported for fsck.py's own `_scan` use and is separate from the 5 back-compat re-exports.
evidence: AC2: 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names'
Plan body: '_ticket_dirs+_resolve_tracker_git_dir move to the leaf (shared by both diagnostic + repair; moving them keeps imports one-way: fsck.py re-imports from the leaf'
Plan body back-compat enumeration names only: '_resolve_tracker_git_dir ... _repair_plan/_repair_ticket/_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES/_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES' (5 total)
fsck.py lines 108, 130, 188, 244 call _ticket_dirs inside _scan; lines 731/736 call it inside _repair_run — both callers span the split boundary
No external consumer of _ticket_dirs imports it from rebar._commands.fsck (grep shows zero hits outside _commands/)
findings.finding: AC2 states 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names' but the plan body names 6 symbols moving to fsck_repair.py that fsck.py must re-import: _resolve_tracker_git_dir, _ticket_dirs (used by _scan at lines 108, 130, 188, 244, 731, 736), _repair_plan, _repair_ticket, _AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES, and _HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES. The count of 5 in AC2 omits _ticket_dirs, which is not an external back-compat symbol but IS required as a re-import for fsck.py's retained _scan function. An implementer verifying only 5 re-imports against AC2 could ship without the _ticket_dirs re-import, breaking every _scan call path silently.
findings.location: AC2 / Plan body 'MOVE to fsck_repair.py' paragraph
scenarios: Implementer adds 5 re-imports to fsck.py (omitting _ticket_dirs), ships the split; _scan immediately raises NameError on first fsck run.
AC5 regression suite catches the NameError at test time, but only if any test exercises _scan — the count discrepancy in AC2 makes the root cause opaque.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC2 to: 'fsck.py re-imports `_ticket_dirs` (for `_scan`) and re-exports the 5 back-compat names (`_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`); the re-export-only four carry `# noqa: F401`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC2: change '5 back-compat names' to '6 back-compat names' (or enumerate them explicitly: `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_ticket_dirs`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`) so the count matches the plan body and implementers add all required `# noqa: F401` re-exports.
evidence: Plan body: '_repair_plan/_repair_ticket/_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES/_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES are re-export-only (test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py)' — that is 4 symbols plus _resolve_tracker_git_dir and _ticket_dirs = 6 total
AC2: 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names' — off by one
tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py:120: 'auto, triage = fsck._AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES, fsck._HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES'
tests/interfaces/store/test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py:125: 'fsck._repair_plan' — confirms 3 of the 4 re-export-only names are directly accessed
findings.finding: AC2 states 'it re-exports the 5 back-compat names' but the plan body names six symbols that move and must be re-exported: `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_ticket_dirs`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`. An implementer trusting AC2's count will omit one re-export, silently breaking `test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py` (which directly accesses `fsck._AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `fsck._HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES`, and `fsck._repair_plan`) — causing AC5 to fail.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 ('re-exports the 5 back-compat names')
scenarios: Implementer counts 5 from AC2 and omits _ticket_dirs (or one of the orphan-type sets) from the re-export block; test_fsck_snapshot_checks.py raises AttributeError on fsck._AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES or fsck._repair_plan, failing AC5.
findings.suggested_fix: AC2: 'fsck.py re-exports the 6 back-compat names: `_resolve_tracker_git_dir`, `_ticket_dirs`, `_repair_plan`, `_repair_ticket`, `_AUTO_RECOVER_ORPHAN_TYPES`, `_HUMAN_TRIAGE_ORPHAN_TYPES` (the last four carry `# noqa: F401`).'
